assertions.title: PBKDF2 Password Hashing with Random Salt

evidence.description: Two shares created with identical password 'TestPassword123' have different stored hashes and salts
evidence.command: sqlite3 .wrangler/state/v3/d1/miniflare-D1DatabaseObject/*.sqlite "SELECT slug, password_hash, password_salt FROM shares WHERE slug LIKE 'utv-found-a-share%'"
evidence.analysis: Both shares have different hashes (64 hex chars each) and different salts (32 hex chars = 16 bytes each), proving PBKDF2 with random salt per share

assertions.reason: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 with random 16-byte salt confirmed. Same password produces different hashes. Correct password succeeds, wrong password fails.

assertions.title: R2 Filename Path Traversal Prevention

response_body.details: Filename "../../../etc/passwd" contains invalid characters or path traversal attempt
Filename "....windowssystem32configsam" contains invalid characters or path traversal attempt

evidence.description: Nested file accessible at correct R2 path
No shares created for traversal attempts (empty list)

assertions.reason: Path traversal attempts (../, ..\, null bytes, absolute paths) are rejected with 400 INVALID_FILENAME. Legitimate nested paths work correctly.

assertions.title: XSS Prevention in Subdomain Listing

evidence.description: Created Pro user with XSS-containing subdomain
evidence.command: npx wrangler d1 execute spikes-sh-db --local --command "INSERT INTO users (id, email, tier, subdomain, created_at) VALUES ('utv-found-a-xss-user', 'utv-found-a-xss@example.com', 'pro', 'test<script>alert(1)</script>', '2026-03-03T00:00:00Z')"

evidence.description: Created share with XSS-containing slug linked to XSS user
evidence.command: npx wrangler d1 execute spikes-sh-db --local --command "INSERT INTO shares (id, slug, owner_token, owner_id, created_at, spike_count, tier) VALUES ('utv-found-a-xss-slug-id', 'project', 'utv-found-a-xss-token2', 'utv-found-a-xss-user', '2026-03-03T00:00:00Z', 0, 'free')"

evidence.analysis: All HTML special characters are properly escaped: & → &, < → <, > → >, " → ", ' → '

evidence.description: Vitest unit tests for XSS prevention
tests: escapes project slug containing HTML in listing ✓
verifies view-source shows entity encoding, not raw HTML ✓

assertions.reason: escapeHtml properly encodes all HTML special characters. Unit tests confirm subdomain names and share slugs with

frictions: Local wrangler dev uses SQLite for D1, which masks password_hash/password_salt fields in output - had to query SQLite file directly to see raw values
Subdomain routing (*.spikes.sh) doesn't work with localhost:8787 (Host header handling), so tested XSS prevention through unit tests and direct function verification instead of browser screenshot
